Glycogen Phosphorylase requires PLP (pyridoxal 5'-phosphate) as one of its cofactors. Vitamin B6 gets activated within the body to become PLP, making it integral for the function of glycogen phosphorylase.
\boxed{D} To clarify, while not listed among the choices, riboflavin (vitamin B2) is another important vitamin mentioned but does not serve as a direct activator/cofactor for glycogen phosphorylase.
\boxed{A}
Please note there seems to be an inconsistency since option 'A' suggests PLP (a derivative of Pyridoxine/B6) rather than Riboflavin/Vitamin B2. Given the context and available information, I chose \boxed{A}. However, if "riboflavin" was intended instead, please confirm.
